factors.summary: Overall demand is stable with mixed evidence.

evidence.text: Total transportation revenues increased 14.3 percent... partially offset by lower rates charged to our customers.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments.

evidence.text: Sourcing net revenue margin for the first quarter of 2017 increased to 9.7 percent from 8.1 percent... but overall net revenue margin decreased.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: Total purchased transportation and related services increased 17.6 percent... due to increased volumes and increased cost of transportation, including fuel.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher headcount.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses increased 4.7 percent... due to an increase in average headcount, including approximately 310 employees added as a result of our acquisition of APC.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives are partially offsetting cost increases.

evidence.text: Compensation programs are performance-based and cash incentive is directly tied to productivity and performance.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: No single contracted carrier represents more than approximately 1.6 percent of our contracted carrier capacity.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Purchased products sourced for resale decreased 14.6 percent... but overall net revenue margin decreased.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: Our customer base is very diverse and unconcentrated... largest customer was approximately two percent of our total revenues.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Our office network... knowledge of local market conditions... important in the transportation industry because it is market driven and very dynamic.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Differentiation is maintained through local market knowledge and customer relationships.

evidence.text: Our office network... knowledge of local market conditions... important in the transportation industry because it is market driven and very dynamic.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Volumes increased in most services, except intermodal which was negatively impacted by truck market competition.

evidence.text: NAST total revenues, including intersegment revenues, increased 12.1 percent to $2.4 billion in the first quarter of 2017 from $2.1 billion in the first quarter of 2016. This increase was driven by volume increases in all services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth was mixed across segments, with North American Surface Transportation declining while Global Forwarding and Robinson Fresh saw increases.

evidence.text: Global Forwarding total revenues, including intersegment revenues, increased 33.5 percent to $476.9 million in the first quarter of 2017 compared to $357.2 million in the first quarter of 2016. This was primarily driven by our acquisition of APC.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

factors.summary: Free cash flow decreased slightly from $104.2 million to $92.7 million, indicating a small decline in operating cash flow.

evidence.text: We generated $92.7 million and $104.2 million of cash flow from operations during the three months ended March 31, 2017 and March 31, 2016, respectively, a decrease of $11.5 million compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: Capital expenditures were slightly lower in 2017 compared to 2016, signaling cautious investment but still focused on growth.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2017, we used $16.7 million for capital expenditures, primarily for facilities, office equipment, and information technology.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: Share repurchases increased in 2017 compared to 2016, indicating management's confidence in the company's future performance.

evidence.text: We used $29.0 million and $21.2 million on share repurchases during the three months ended March 31, 2017 and March 31, 2016. The change was due to an increase in the number of shares repurchased and the average price of the repurchased shares during the three months ended March 31, 2017, compared to the same period of 2016.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: The acquisition of APC contributed to revenue growth, particularly in Global Forwarding.

evidence.text: These increases were primarily driven by our acquisition of APC.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

factors.summary: The company experienced pricing pressure, with a decline in pricing power particularly in truckload services, as evidenced by lower customer pricing and reduced net revenue margins.

evidence.text: The increase was driven by increased volumes in all of our transportation services, partially offset by lower rates charged to our customers.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: NAST truckload net revenue margin decreased in the first quarter of 2017 compared to the first quarter of 2016, due primarily to lower customer pricing, including fuel costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

factors.summary: Cost actions had a mixed impact on expenses, with some cost reduction initiatives contributing to savings but partially offsetting increases in other areas.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses were partially offset by decreased expenses related to incentive plans... Other selling, general, and administrative expenses increased... partially offset by the collection of a previously resolved legal claim.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: NAST operating expenses decreased 2.2 percent in the first quarter of 2017 to $216.6 million compared to $221.4 million in the first quarter of 2016. This decrease was due to the collection of a previously resolved legal claim of $8.75 million as well as decreases in personnel expenses.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

factors.summary: Margins are compressing, driven by a decline in gross margins due to lower pricing and higher transportation costs, as well as decreases in income from operations in key segments.

evidence.text: Our transportation net revenue margin decreased to 17.3 percent... Sourcing net revenue margin increased... but overall net revenue margin decreased.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: Income from operations decreased 5.5 percent... primarily driven by declines in income from operations in NAST and Robinson Fresh.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: NAST net revenues decreased 3.0 percent to $372.4 million in the first quarter of 2017 from $383.8 million in the first quarter of 2016. This decrease was driven by a decline in truckload net revenues, discussed below.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2017 , Compared to Three Months Ended March 31, 2016
